mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and PKC signaling to reduce proliferation/survival in FLT3-driven AML
additional_notes: AML-approved; described as PKC/FLT3 multi-kinase inhibitor

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL receptor tyrosine kinases to block AML growth signaling
additional_notes: AML-approved

mechanism: Selective inhibition of FLT3 signaling to suppress FLT3-driven AML proliferation
additional_notes: Clinical/approved in some regions

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3 to reduce AML cell proliferation/survival
additional_notes: Repurposed off-label in FLT3-ITD AML

mechanism: Inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases with reported FLT3 activity
additional_notes: Includes FLT3 activity

mechanism: Broad kinase inhibition; reported activity in some FLT3-driven contexts
additional_notes: Activity noted in some FLT3-driven contexts

mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2 to trigger mitochondrial apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: AML-approved in combinations; often steep dose–response so include very low nM points

mechanism: Inhibits anti-apoptotic BCL-2 family proteins (BCL-2 and BCL-xL) to promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Also referred to as ABT-263; thrombocytopenia limits clinical use but useful preclinically

mechanism: Gossypol-derived compound with BH3 mimetic-like pro-apoptotic activity and multiple targets
additional_notes: Alternative name: gossypol; noted as multiple targets

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway to suppress proliferation in MAPK-active AML subsets
additional_notes: Described as effective in MAPK-active AML subsets

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter chromatin/transcription and reduce tumor cell viability
additional_notes: Hematologic activity; potent in vitro

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to change gene expression and induce growth arrest/apoptosis
additional_notes: Also referred to as SAHA

mechanism: Potent HDAC inhibition leading to transcriptional changes and apoptosis
additional_notes: Noted as very potent

mechanism: Weak HDAC inhibition with epigenetic effects; often requires high concentrations in vitro
additional_notes: Weak potency; frequently requires mM concentrations in vitro

mechanism: Incorporates into nucleic acids and inhibits DNMT activity to reduce methylation and alter gene expression
additional_notes: AML-approved; needs longer exposure; note suggests 96 h exposure or repeated dosing to model clinical schedule

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNMTs causing DNA hypomethylation and transcriptional reprogramming
additional_notes: AML/MDS-approved; often more potent than azacitidine in vitro; note suggests 96 h exposure

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome to induce proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Cited with multiple AML studies; suggested positive control

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome to trigger proteostasis stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Noted as potent

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase to increase oxidative stress and promote cell death
additional_notes: Induces oxidative stress; activity reported in AML models

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and inhibits autophagy/lysosomal function, sometimes enhancing AML cell death
additional_notes: Modest as single agent; sometimes enhances AML cell death
